metadata.title:
SECOND BIG FARMERS =


RALLY ON SATURDAY

pages.raw_text:
The second rally of Spokane
county farmers in support of the
grand jury investigation of county
affairs will be held at the court
house next Saturday, beginning at
10 a. m.


Posters in red ink have been
hung at various points throughout
the county urging the farmers to
come out, and good attendance
is expected because of the fact that
the bulk of the harvest rush is
now over. A delegation is coming
from Green Bluff with the farm
ers' band of that section.


An invitation has been sent to
J. B. Kegley, master of the state
grange, to be on hand and address
the meeting. The making of ar
rangements is in charge of a com.
mittee appointed at the last grand
jury rally, of which R. B. Martin
of Opportunity is the chairman.


The presbytery of the Presby-
terrain church, which will be in
session here next Saturday, will be
asked to adjourn in order to at
tend the meeting, as the Presby
terian ministers of the city have
evinced a disposition to back the
probe of county affairs.


At it's last meeting the minis
terial association of Spokane adopt
ed a resolution thanking R. B. Mar
tin for the work he had done in
behalf of the county investigation.


At the coming meeting steps
will be taken to form an organiza-
